title: BMBL Stock Earnings: Bumble Beats EPS, Misses Revenue for Q2 2024
text: Bumble Inc. reported mixed Q2 2024 earnings results where the company beat analyst estimates for earnings per share but missed on revenue. In Q2 2024, Bumble reported earnings per share of 22 cents, which was above the analyst estimate for EPS of 13 cents, representing a 69% beat. However, the company's revenue performance was less impressive, missing analyst expectations. Total revenue growth was tempered at 3% year-over-year, driven by user growth of 14% but offset by an 8% decline in average revenue per paying user. Foreign exchange headwinds accounted for approximately 1.5 percentage points of the revenue growth shortfall. The Bumble app showed positive momentum with 5% revenue growth, driven by a 15% increase in paying users. However, the Badoo app and other segments declined 2% year-over-year. The company provided revised full-year 2024 guidance, with expectations for at least 200 basis points of adjusted EBITDA margin expansion. This mixed performance highlighted the company's profitability improvements despite monetization headwinds.
